name: PayForward
peopleReachedDefinition: Direct count of unique individual clients served through PayForward lending and savings products. Equals the 'Total Clients Served' output indicator — each client counted once at first product access.
householdsReachedDefinition: Estimated as approximately 71% of total clients served, based on household deduplication analysis. Multiple clients from the same household are counted once. Based on national ID cross-referencing.
jobsCreatedDefinition: Full-time equivalent (FTE) jobs directly created by PayForward: 82 loan officers, 56 branch and back-office staff, 40 mobile banking agents. Verified through payroll and agent commission records.

outputs.label: Total Clients Served
outputs.methodology: Unique client accounts with at least one active loan or savings product, deduplicated by national ID

outputs.label: Loans Disbursed
outputs.fullName: Number of Micro and Small Loans Disbursed
outputs.methodology: Cumulative count of loans disbursed since fund inception (includes repeat borrowers)

outputs.label: Savings Accounts Opened
outputs.methodology: Cumulative new savings accounts opened with minimum initial deposit since inception

outputs.label: Households Reached
outputs.fullName: Unique households with at least one active financial product
outputs.methodology: Approximately 71% of individual clients represent unique households, based on household surveys
outputs.source: Client database with household deduplication surveys

outputs.label: Jobs Created
outputs.fullName: Direct full-time equivalent jobs created through PayForward operations
outputs.methodology: Count of full-time equivalent positions, verified quarterly through payroll records

outcomes.label: Repayment Rate
outcomes.fullName: Loan Repayment Rate (On-Time or Within Grace Period)
outcomes.methodology: Portfolio at Risk (PAR30) methodology — percentage of outstanding loans with no payments more than 30 days overdue

outcomes.label: Household Income Increase
outcomes.fullName: Average Household Income Increase Among Active Borrowers
outcomes.methodology: Lean Data phone surveys using I4DI Living Income methodology, comparing income at loan application vs 12 months post-disbursement (n=500)

outcomes.label: Financial Resilience
outcomes.fullName: Clients Able to Cover Unexpected Expense Without Borrowing
outcomes.methodology: Annual resilience survey adapted from FinScope toolkit — measures ability to cover a 50,000 NGN unexpected expense within 30 days without new borrowing

outcomes.label: Women Beneficiary %
outcomes.fullName: Percentage of total clients who are female
outcomes.methodology: Calculated from sex-disaggregated client registration data

financials.label: Revenue
financials.fullName: Semi-annual gross revenue from loan interest income, transaction fees, and premium service subscriptions
financials.methodology: Gross revenue on accrual basis per IFRS 15. Includes loan interest income on outstanding micro and small loan portfolio, transaction fees from mobile money and agent cash-in/cash-out, and premium service subscription fees.
financials.source: Audited financial statements and management accounts

financials.label: Valuation
financials.fullName: Fair market value of the fund's equity position
financials.methodology: Fair market value via DCF and comparable transactions.
financials.source: Independent valuation report and management estimate

financials.label: Capital Deployed
financials.fullName: Total committed capital called and deployed

profile.description: PayForward is a digital financial services platform providing microloans, savings products, and insurance to underserved populations in Nigeria. The platform uses alternative credit scoring powered by mobile usage data and transaction history to serve customers excluded from traditional banking.
profile.theoryOfChange: By providing affordable, accessible digital financial services to unbanked and underbanked populations, PayForward enables household financial resilience, supports micro-enterprise growth, and reduces vulnerability to economic shocks. Access to savings and insurance products builds long-term financial security, while microcredit enables productive investment in education, health, and small business development.
profile.targetBeneficiaries: Unbanked and underbanked adults in urban and peri-urban Nigeria, including market traders, gig workers, and smallholder farmers. Minimum 45% women borrowers target.
profile.geographicFocus: Lagos, Ogun, Oyo, Kano, and Rivers states. Concentrated in urban corridors with expansion into secondary cities. Agent network covers 24 local government areas.
profile.implementationApproach: Fully digital lending platform with USSD and mobile app channels. Agent banking network for cash-in/cash-out. AI-driven credit scoring using alternative data (mobile money transactions, utility payments, social network analysis).
profile.reportingFrequency: Semi-annual (H1/H2) impact reports. Monthly financial reporting to fund management. Quarterly portfolio-at-risk reporting.
